references.title: A three learning states Bayesian knowledge tracing model

extracted.Students:
912 students - Assistments Math 2004-2005

3136 students - Assistments Math 2005-2006

5046 students -  e Assistments
Math 2006–2007
extracted.Flaw of Previous Models: BKT is based on Hiden Markov Models, where the observations are the answers to previous questions (correct or incorrect).  This bi-partition is not always clear.
extracted.Novelty of Model:
The authors introduce a tripartite of representing each element of the BKT sequence:

Unlearned State/Learning State/Learned State
extracted.Preprocessing Details:
Students with fewer than 100 learning processes were excluded.

Each learner is usually represented by the sequence of exercises within a knowledge component - a binary of learned state and unlearned state.

Authors simulate the bipartite vs the tripartite data representation using synthetic data - with results being clearly better for TPS.

Each learner is ultimately represented by the sequences in each of the 5 considered knowledge components.
extracted.Comments:
Before going into the real datasets, the authors test the performance of both traditional BKT and TLS-BKT on random subsets of the datasets.

One model is created for each individual knowledge component.

Here, they consider multiple metrics beyond the AUC score: Distinction, Accuracy, RMSE and Standard Deviation.

TLS-BKT outperforms BKT on every benchmark test.

Need to validate, but it seems that there is one model per knowledge component, which would make such a model very difficult to create in practice.
extracted.Limitations:
Lacks generalization to environments outside Assistments Math.

Evaluation on the full real data does not consider all metrics used for the randomly selected data data.

annotations.text: Traditional BKT has two-learning states and the authors propose a third learning state. This means that they define the learning process into 3 sections. This small change to BKT shows superior results in comparison with BKT both in quantitative and qualitative terms.
Representation of the bipartitate state of the sequences vs the tri-partite state.
